---
title: "Compute performance"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Compute performance}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(l1spectralclustering)
```

In this vignette, we describe how to use the function `Compute_performance()`. This function evaluates the performance of a clustering algorithm (e.g., the $\ell_1$-clustering algorithm) by comparing its output against the ground-truth adjacency matrix.


```{r}
# Example: loading one simulated data set and its corresponding learned result

# Define simulation parameters
n <- 50
d <- 0.1
p_inside <- 0.1
p_outside <- 0.25

# Construct the filenames and paths
data_path <- file.path(path.package("l1spectralclustering"), "data")
results_path <- file.path(path.package("l1spectralclustering"), "results")
file_suffix <- sprintf("n=%d_density=%.2f_p_inside=%.2f_p_outside=%.2f.Rdata", n, d, p_inside, p_outside)
data_file    <- file.path(data_path, paste0("data_", file_suffix))
results_file <- file.path(results_path, paste0("results_", file_suffix))

# Load the simulated data
if (file.exists(data_file)) {
  load(data_file)
  data_example <- graphs[[1]]
} else {
  stop("Simulated data file not found at: ", data_file)
}

# Load the clustering results
if (file.exists(results_file)) {
  load(results_file)
  res_example <- Results[[1]]
} else {
  warning("Clustering results not found at: ", results_file, ". You may need to run the algorithm first.")
}

# Convert membership matrix to vector, assigning 0 to nodes with no membership
clus_vector <- apply(res_example$comm, 1, function(row) {
  if (all(row == 0)) {
    return(0) 
  } else {
    return(which.max(row))
  }
})

# Compute performance
results <- ComputePerformance(clus_vector, data_example$A)
```

Add the generation of the figures. Also add an example where we use another algorithm.
